title: TopicClusterer returns exemplar indices alongside text
description:
**As a** memory analysis pipeline, **I want** the TopicClusterer to return the dataset indices of representative documents alongside the text, **so that** calling code can look up the corresponding Item IDs and identifiers for each exemplar.

## Background

`TopicClusterer._get_representative_docs(topic_id, n)` currently returns `List[str]` — the text of the n documents closest to the cluster centroid. The calling code in `VectorTopicMemory` has a parallel `doc_ids` list where `doc_ids[i]` corresponds to `documents[i]`, but the clusterer discards the indices after selecting representative docs. Without the indices, there is no way to trace an exemplar back to its source Item.

## Solution

Add `_get_representative_doc_indices(topic_id, n)` that returns `List[int]` — the indices into the original documents list for the n most representative documents. The existing `_get_representative_docs` can be reimplemented in terms of this method. The caller then uses `doc_ids[index]` to get the Item ID.

## Key Files

- Modify: `plexus/analysis/topic_clusterer.py`
- Modify: `plexus/analysis/topic_clusterer_test.py`

## Dependencies

None. First story in the epic.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
Feature: TopicClusterer returns exemplar indices

  Scenario: Representative doc indices map to the original documents list
    Given a TopicClusterer fitted with 100 documents and their embeddings
    And topic 0 has 20 member documents
    When _get_representative_doc_indices(topic_id=0, n=5) is called
    Then 5 integer indices are returned
    And each index is in the range 0 to 99
    And documents[index] for each index is a member of topic 0

  Scenario: Indices are ordered by proximity to centroid
    Given a TopicClusterer fitted with documents and embeddings
    And topic 0 has a known centroid
    When _get_representative_doc_indices(topic_id=0, n=3) is called
    Then the first index corresponds to the document closest to the centroid
    And the second index is the next closest
    And the third index is the next closest

  Scenario: _get_representative_docs returns the same texts as indexed lookup
    Given a TopicClusterer fitted with documents
    When _get_representative_doc_indices(topic_id=0, n=5) returns indices
    And _get_representative_docs(topic_id=0, n=5) returns texts
    Then documents[indices[i]] equals texts[i] for all i

  Scenario: Requesting more exemplars than members returns all members
    Given a topic with only 3 member documents
    When _get_representative_doc_indices(topic_id, n=5) is called
    Then 3 indices are returned

  Scenario: Empty or missing topic returns empty list
    Given a topic_id that has no members
    When _get_representative_doc_indices(topic_id, n=5) is called
    Then an empty list is returned
